$comment: Locale fallback-chain experiments. Each scenario creates a set of locales (idempotently) and entries within them, then optionally deletes locales in a specific order to test cma-api's behavior. Designed to drive variety on the metering pipeline (entry_created in non-master locales, entries_orphaned_by_locale_deleted on delete). NOT included in the periodic cron — opt-in via CONTENTSTACK_RUN_LOCALE_EXPERIMENTS=1.

scenarios.description: Build a 3-level fallback chain: cs-cz → de-de → en-us. Create entries in each level. Don't delete — just exercise the create path.
scenarios.$comment-locales: Assumes de-de already exists (created by seed-locales-branches). cs-cz creation expects de-de to exist as fallback.

scenarios.description: Create a leaf locale es-mx with fallback en-us; create entries; delete es-mx. Drives entries_orphaned_by_locale_deleted for the localized entries.

deletePlan.$note: Leaf delete should succeed; orphans the entries that only existed in es-mx

scenarios.description: Build chain it-it → en-us, populate, delete the leaf, then recreate. Tests that deleted-then-recreated locales come back empty.

Build chain pt-pt → es-mx → en-us, populate at every level, then attempt to delete the MIDDLE locale (es-mx) while pt-pt still depends on it. Tests whether cma-api blocks this OR auto-reparents pt-pt's fallback to en-us. Empirical — outcome TBD.

deletePlan.$note: Might 422 with 'locale has dependents' or might silently reparent pt-pt to en-us. Inspect the error body.
After the middle either delete or not, leaf should always delete cleanly
